--- title: "[data.load_dataset()]{.doc-object-name .doc-function .doc-label .doc-label-function}" body-classes: doc-api-page page-navigation: false html-table-processing: none --- ::: {.doc-subject} Load a bundled dataset by name. ::: ::: {.doc-usage-source} Usage [Source](https://github.com/rich-iannone/greenwood/blob/main/greenwood/data/__init__.py#L80-L136){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} ::: ::: {.doc-signature .doc-Kind.FUNCTION} ```python data.load_dataset(name, *, backend=None) ``` ::: ::: {.doc-text} Greenwood ships several classic survival-analysis datasets from R's `survival` package, stored as gzipped CSVs. This function decompresses them on the fly and returns a DataFrame in your preferred backend. ::: ## Parameters {.doc-parameters} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [name]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [str]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : One of `available_datasets()` (e.g., `"lung"`, `"veteran"`, `"ovarian"`, `"pbc"`, `"colon"`, `"mgus2"`). [backend]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [str | None]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [None]{.doc-parameter-default} : `"pandas"` or `"polars"`. When left as `None` (the default), Greenwood picks a backend for you: it prefers Polars if it is installed, otherwise uses Pandas, and raises if neither is available. ::: ## Returns {.doc-returns} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [DataFrame]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : A Polars or Pandas DataFrame with the dataset contents. ::: ## Examples {.doc-examples} Load the NCCTG lung cancer dataset as a Polars DataFrame: ::: {#28cd7f36 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import greenwood as gw # Load the NCCTG lung cancer dataset as a Polars DataFrame lung = gw.load_dataset("lung", backend="polars") lung.head() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ```{=html}
shape: (5, 10)
insttimestatusagesexph.ecogph.karnopat.karnomeal.calwt.loss

31010156109090null15
5210257119060115011
18832601010090null0
``` ::: ::: See all available dataset names: ::: {#b86cee6d .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} # List all available dataset names gw.available_datasets() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ``` ['colon', 'lung', 'mgus2', 'ovarian', 'pbc', 'veteran'] ``` ::: :::
